sections.summary: This summary provides key information about the PayEUR e-money token. It is intended to support the prospective holder in making an informed decision. The summary covers the issuer as an authorised electronic money institution, the par-value redemption right of the holder, the principal risks, the safeguarding of funds received against issuance, and the technology underlying the token. The full white paper must be read alongside this summary.
sections.risk_warning: The e-money token described in this white paper may lose its value in part or in full, may not always be transferable, and may not be liquid. The token is not covered by an investor compensation scheme or a deposit guarantee scheme. The risk of loss is real and the holder should not commit funds that cannot be lost in full.
sections.management_statement: The management body of PayEUR EMI GmbH declares that this white paper complies with the requirements of Regulation (EU) 2023/1114 (MiCAR), that the information presented is fair, clear, and not misleading, and that no information has been omitted that would be material to the assessment of the e-money token. The management body assumes responsibility for the content of this white paper.
sections.notification_date: Date of notification to BaFin: 1 July 2026.
sections.language: This white paper is drawn up in English, a language customary in the sphere of international finance, and a German-language translation is available on the issuer's portal.
sections.issuer: PayEUR EMI GmbH is the issuer of the e-money token. PayEUR EMI GmbH is a limited liability company organised under the laws of the Federal Republic of Germany, with its registered seat at Kaiserstraße 12, 60311 Frankfurt am Main, registered under HRB 098455. The issuer holds an authorisation as an electronic money institution under the Zahlungsdiensteaufsichtsgesetz, which authorisation is recognised for the purposes of Art. 48(1)(b) MiCAR. The issuer is registered with BaFin under registration number 12345678.
sections.emt: The PayEUR is an e-money token within the meaning of Art. 3(1)(7) MiCAR. The referenced official currency is the euro. Each PayEUR unit is denominated as one euro and is issued at par against funds received in euro. The token is intended for use as a means of payment, settlement, and treasury management.
sections.offer_or_admission: The offer is addressed to professional, eligible counterparty, and retail customers in selected European Union jurisdictions for which the issuer has completed the passporting notification. The issuance is conducted at par against funds received in euro plus the applicable transaction fees. The target market includes payment institutions, merchants accepting on-chain settlement, and consumers seeking euro-denominated digital cash.
sections.rights_and_obligations: The holder has the right to redeem the e-money token at par value against the issuer at any time, with no minimum threshold, in accordance with Art. 49(3) MiCAR. The holder has no right to interest or any benefit linked to the duration of holding the token. The obligations of the holder include compliance with applicable transfer restrictions, sanctions screening, and the issuer's terms of service. The redemption right cannot be waived by contract.
sections.technology: The PayEUR is issued on a permissioned distributed ledger using a Byzantine fault tolerant consensus mechanism. Smart contract modules govern issuance, redemption, and freeze functionality. Custody of the underlying technical keys is provided by a qualified custodian under a multi-party computation key management arrangement. The issuance protocol has been audited by two independent code security firms.
sections.risks: The principal risks include market risk on the underlying euro funds, credit risk on the safeguarding credit institution, liquidity risk during a redemption surge, operational risk including smart contract failure or key compromise, redemption risk during operational emergencies, safeguarding risk should the safeguarding arrangement fail, and regulatory risk including changes in MiCAR or EMD2 Level 2 standards. Each risk is described in detail in the dedicated risk section of this white paper.
sections.environmental_impact: The consensus mechanism is a permissioned Byzantine fault tolerant variant that does not rely on proof-of-work and does not consume energy at the level of public proof-of-work chains. The issuer publishes annual environmental and climate-related impact data in line with the ESMA regulatory technical standards on sustainability indicators for distributed ledger technology. Validators are hosted on European mainland data centres.
sections.safeguarding: Funds received in exchange for issuance of the e-money token are safeguarded in accordance with Art. 7 of Directive 2009/110/EC (EMD2) as referenced by Art. 54 MiCAR. The funds are held on a segregated client money account at a Tier-1 credit institution authorised in the European Union, are not commingled with the issuer's own funds, and are protected from claims of the issuer's creditors in the event of the issuer's insolvency.
